ENGENIX Operating Command

What needs action now, why it matters, and who owns the next move.

The first screen after login supervises every active deal from Desk through Finalization. It does not replace the operating workspaces. It directs the operator to the exact decision, evidence gap, customer concern, cash risk, or obligation requiring attention.

Manager Attention Queue
Cross-layer actions ordered by customer, cash, compliance, and timing impact
Accountability
Owners and response windows currently driving execution
LIVE
Deal Lifecycle Visibility
Where work is accumulating and which deal is creating the current stage pressure
Continuous Deal Reconciliation
The same deal compared across customer expectation, desk, lender, F&I, contract, funding, and office records
Operating Thesis
The command layer supervises the work without hiding its source
One deal. One decision thread.

Every structure, product choice, signature, stipulation, customer promise, refund, and final accounting result remains attached to the same operating record.

Escalate with context

Operators enter the source workspace with the exact deal, evidence gap, owner, and recommended next move already selected.

Protect the truth

ENGENIX flags drift between what the customer expected, what the lender approved, what F&I sold, what the contract states, and what accounting finally records.

Continuous Deal Reconciliation

Protect the deal’s truth from first pencil through final accounting.

ENGENIX compares the controlling values and customer choices at every handoff, then routes each mismatch to the correct operator before it becomes a funding delay, compliance exposure, gross leak, or customer dispute.

Open Cross-Layer Mismatches
Synthetic demonstration records with source, impact, owner, and resolution path
Source Comparison
Select a mismatch to compare the source records
SELECT ITEM
Cross-Workspace Escalations

Leadership attention arrives with evidence, ownership, and a recommended next move.

No mystery alerts. Every escalation explains what changed, why the condition matters, who currently owns it, the response window, and which workspace contains the operating action.

Leadership Decision Queue
Escalations retain their source workspace and deal identity
Multi-Rooftop Operating Visibility

See where execution is breaking before a monthly report explains the damage.

Compare rooftop pressure across wait time, gross at risk, compliance exposure, funding holds, customer recovery, and unfinished obligations using the same operating definitions.

Cross-Rooftop Pattern Signals
Synthetic operating observations, not legal or statistical conclusions
90 DAY DEMO
Funding packet rework is concentrated at Summit Motors

Three of four current funding holds originate from incomplete customer evidence at the F&I handoff.

Premier Ford preserves more gross during rehash

Collateral rescue is used earlier, reducing average front-gross concession in the synthetic outcome history.

Velocity Cars has the longest title aging

Two payoff-verification exceptions account for most title delay currently visible in Finalization.

Premier Honda resolves customer concerns fastest

Ownership Concierge recovery alerts are acknowledged before menu presentation in the current demo records.

Unified Deal Memory

Every decision survives the handoff.

The deal thread preserves customer expectations, manager pencils, lender callbacks, product choices, compliance dispositions, funding evidence, refunds, promises, and final economics in one durable record.

Morgan Davis · FI-8421 / DX-7314
Synthetic unified lifecycle thread across the ENGENIX operating layer
ACTIVE RECORD
Controller Intelligence · Operating Close

Turn every operating decision into trusted financial truth.

Reconcile expected economics, funding proceeds, receivables, payables, payroll, accruals, chargebacks, customer obligations, and statement readiness without replacing the DMS or the controller’s judgment.

Deal-to-ledger traceability Timing versus true leakage Explainable close readiness
Operating Close Pipeline
From delivered deal through funded, posted, accrued, reconciled, and statement-ready
Controller Exception Queue
Ordered by statement impact, cash exposure, customer liability, aging, and ownership
Financial Exposure
Current amounts requiring proof, posting, accrual, or ownership
Close Readiness

Know whether the statement can be trusted before the calendar says the month is over.

Readiness is built from explainable controls across funding, cash, title, refunds, AP, AR, payroll, accruals, deal-to-ledger reconciliation, and controller signoff.

Blocking the Close
Controls that prevent controller signoff or reduce statement confidence
Department Readiness
Completion and exposure by operating owner
Controller Exception Queue

Separate timing differences from true leakage before they blur together at month end.

Each exception carries financial impact, source evidence, statement treatment, owner, age, and a defined path to resolution.

Financial Exceptions
Synthetic queue spanning deals, cash, liabilities, payroll, AP, AR, and posting controls
Controlled Variable

Trace variable gross from the first pencil to realized financial outcome.

Controlled Variable explains where value moved, who absorbed the change, whether it is timing or leakage, and which source record supports the conclusion.

Variable Gross Bridge
Expected to realized, with every economic movement classified
Root-Cause Ledger
What changed, why, and whether the effect is temporary or permanent
Controller Decision
Accept, assign, accrue, or reopen the operating record
Deal-to-Ledger Reconciliation

Connect every booked number to the operating decision that created it.

Compare accepted desk economics, F&I results, lender proceeds, DMS posting, and realized gross without asking the controller to reconstruct the story by hand.

Deal Reconciliation Ledger
Synthetic source-level comparison with statement treatment
DealExpectedBookedRealizedVarianceClassificationOwnerAction
Accounts Receivable Intelligence

Every receivable should have a source, owner, aging clock, and expected resolution.

Funding shortages, manufacturer money, customer balances, cancellations, warranty receivables, and dealer trades stay connected to the operation that created them.

Receivable Exception Ledger
Synthetic balances prioritized by age, collectability, and statement impact
SourceReferenceAmountAgeExpectedOwnerStatus
Accounts Payable Intelligence

Match every expense to the deal, promise, product, vendor, or operating decision behind it.

Surface unmatched invoices, duplicate risk, unapproved spend, We-Owe costs, recon overruns, and product remittance discrepancies before close.

Payable Exception Ledger
Synthetic AP exceptions tied back to operating records
Vendor / SourceReferenceAmountIssueAccruedOwnerStatus
Payroll & Commission Reconciliation

Pay the plan against realized economics, not a stale snapshot of the deal.

Compare pay-plan rules, splits, units, front gross, back gross, chargebacks, cancellations, and posted payroll while preserving controller authority.

Commission Exceptions
Synthetic pay-plan calculations for demonstration only
EmployeeRoleReferenceCalculatedPostedVarianceReasonStatus
Accruals & Liabilities

Make unfinished obligations financially visible before they become next month’s surprise.

We-Owes, customer refunds, chargebacks, unpaid vendor work, product remittance, title exposure, and policy expense remain visible until accrued, paid, or resolved.

Open Liability Register
Estimated, verified, accrued, and paid are kept separate
Statement Readiness

Explain the developing statement before leadership reads the final number.

Bridge operating volume, front gross, F&I performance, personnel expense, policy, chargebacks, accruals, and unresolved variances into one controller-reviewed view.

Statement Bridge
Operating expectation to current statement estimate
Controller Narrative
Explainable drivers behind the month
Department Statement Readiness
Synthetic current-period financial operating view
DepartmentPlanCurrentVariancePrimary DriverConfidence
Month-End Checklist

A close is complete only when every material control has evidence and an accountable owner.

Run the operating close in sequence, preserve signoff, and keep reopened controls visible instead of burying them in email and spreadsheets.

Operating Close Controls
Click any control to record or reopen synthetic completion
Controller Close Brief

Give ownership a clean explanation of what closed, what moved, and what remains exposed.

The brief converts controller work into a leadership-ready operating narrative without exposing private source documents or replacing the financial statement.

Contracts in Transit

Turn every funding delay into an owned, timed operating action.

Funding status, shortages, lender communication, aging, and missing evidence stay attached to the deal until proceeds are received and reconciled.

CIT Aging & Shortage Queue
Synthetic funding records ordered by age and cash exposure
Title & Trade Payoff

Close ownership transfer without losing the trail.

Track payoff verification, title receipt, lien release, state packet, trade identity, and customer follow-up as one accountable closeout process.

Title and Payoff Exceptions
Open source evidence, owner, aging, and next action
Cancellations & Customer Refunds

Estimated value is not completed value.

Separate estimated cancellation amounts, administrator verification, funds received, payoff treatment, customer refund, and final accounting evidence.

Cancellation and Refund Ledger
No value is treated as completed until the administrator and accounting record agree
Customer Promise Control

Every We-Owe and dealership commitment remains visible until fulfilled.

Owner, due date, customer communication, evidence, and completion status stay attached to the delivered deal.

Open Customer Commitments
Synthetic We-Owe, second-key, accessory, and refund obligations
Controlled Variable · Deal Economics

See exactly where expected variable gross changed before it reached the statement.

Trace desk gross through contracted, funded, booked, and realized economics. Every variance is classified as timing, posting error, liability, shortage, or true leakage.

Controlled Variable Bridge
Expected, contracted, funded, booked, and realized values with source-level explanation
Closed Periods & Deal Archive

Every accepted close becomes searchable financial operating memory.

Final funding, title, cancellation, promises, product remittance, and accounting results remain attached to the deal for audit, pattern learning, and future customer recognition.

Recently Completed Records
Synthetic closed-deal and prior-period outcomes
DealCustomerFundedTitleRefundsPromisesFinal Gross
Controller Intelligence Controls

Configure the evidence, ownership, thresholds, and authority required to close.

These demonstration controls show how each dealership could define aging thresholds, ownership rules, escalation windows, and completion evidence.

Dealer-Controlled Rules
Synthetic sandbox settings only
Escalate contracts in transit after three business days
Adds the deal to Operating Command and the Leadership Briefing.
Require administrator verification before refund completion
Estimated cancellation values remain pending until supported.
Block completed status while customer promises remain open
We-Owes and written commitments cannot disappear at delivery.
Reconcile final gross against desk and F&I expectations
Variance thresholds can require controller review.

Compliance Copilot · explainable operating intelligence

ENGENIX converts detected variances into proposed findings, dealer-approved response paths, consequence previews, owned remediation, and preserved operating memory.

Product Fit

AI-generated customer ownership infographic

AI Ownership Brief

A premium, tailored presentation that explains the ownership decision, preserves individual prices, and supports a transparent upgrade conversation.

Customer-choice boundary: This tool explains optional products and value differences. It does not hide pricing, imply that a product is required for financing, or finalize a selection without an affirmative customer choice.
Transparent customer choice

Customer Presentation

Record what was offered, each individual price, what the customer selected or declined, and any change after presentation.

Presented Products

F&I workspace controls

Settings

Dealer-controlled guardrails for product fit, pricing, customer choice, and downstream review.

Decision-Support Guardrails
Keep F&I recommendations explainable and voluntary
Require individual product pricing
Every product remains visible and individually selectable during presentation.
Block duplicate active coverage
Require manager review when a new product overlaps transferable or active coverage.
Require optional-product acknowledgment
Preserve that optional products were presented as voluntary and individually priced.
Separate estimated and verified cancellations
Never treat a preliminary refund estimate as immediately available customer funds.
